classifications.phrase: decided to increase the cash rate target by 25 basis points
it picked up materially in the second half of 2025
some of the increase in inflation reflects greater capacity pressures
inflation is likely to remain above target for some time
Growth in private demand has strengthened substantially more than expected
Activity and prices in the housing market are also continuing to pick up
labour market conditions remain a little tight
The unemployment rate has been a little lower than expected
broader measures of wages growth continue to be strong
growth in unit labour costs remains high
inflationary pressures picked up materially in the second half of 2025
private demand is growing more quickly than expected
capacity pressures are greater than previously assessed
it was appropriate to increase the cash rate target

payload.rationale: The statement signals a hawkish policy stance across inflation, growth, and employment dimensions, with the RBA raising rates due to material pickup in inflation, stronger-than-expected demand growth, and tight labour market conditions.
